heading: Denial of assistance and benefits for certain drug-related convictions
label: 862a Denial of assistance and benefits for certain drug-related convictions

children.text: assistance under any State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], or42 U.S.C. 601

benefits under the supplemental nutrition assistance program (as defined in section 3 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ()) or any State program carried out under that Act [ et seq.].7 U.S.C. 20127 U.S.C. 2011

children.heading: Effects on assistance and benefits for others
children.label: b Effects on assistance and benefits for others

children.heading: Program of temporary assistance for needy families
children.label: 1 Program of temporary assistance for needy families
children.text: The amount of assistance otherwise required to be provided under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq.] to the family members of an individual to whom subsection (a) applies shall be reduced by the amount which would have otherwise been made available to the individual under such part.42 U.S.C. 601

children.heading: Benefits under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008
children.label: 2 Benefits under the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008
children.text: The amount of benefits otherwise required to be provided to a household under the supplemental nutrition assistance program (as defined in section 3 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 ()), or any State program carried out under that Act [ et seq.], shall be determined by considering the individual to whom subsection (a) applies not to be a member of such household, except that the income and resources of the individual shall be considered to be income and resources of the household.7 U.S.C. 20127 U.S.C. 2011

children.heading: Enforcement
children.label: c Enforcement
children.text: A State that has not exercised its authority under subsection (d)(1)(A) shall require each individual applying for assistance or benefits referred to in subsection (a), during the application process, to state, in writing, whether the individual, or any member of the household of the individual, has been convicted of a crime described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Opt out
children.label: A Opt out
children.text: A State may, by specific reference in a law enacted after , exempt any or all individuals domiciled in the State from the application of subsection (a).August 22, 1996

children.heading: Limit period of prohibition
children.label: B Limit period of prohibition
children.text: A State may, by law enacted after , limit the period for which subsection (a) shall apply to any or all individuals domiciled in the State.August 22, 1996

children.heading: Inapplicability to convictions occurring on or beforeAugust 22, 1996
children.label: 2 Inapplicability to convictions occurring on or beforeAugust 22, 1996
children.text: Subsection (a) shall not apply to a conviction if the conviction is for conduct occurring on or before .August 22, 1996

in section 419(5) of the Social Security Act [], when referring to assistance provided under a State program funded under part A of title IV of the Social Security Act [ et seq.], and42 U.S.C. 619(5)42 U.S.C. 601

in section 3 of the Food and Nutrition Act of 2008 (), when referring to the supplemental nutrition assistance program (as defined in that section) or any State program carried out under that Act [ et seq.].7 U.S.C. 20127 U.S.C. 2011
Emergency medical services under title XIX of the Social Security Act [ et seq.].42 U.S.C. 1396
Short-term, noncash, in-kind emergency disaster relief.

children.label: B
children.text: Public health assistance for testing and treatment of communicable diseases if the Secretary of Health and Human Services determines that it is necessary to prevent the spread of such disease.
